title: Meditation vs thinking task
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from participants performing alternating meditation and active thinking tasks. Data were collected at the Meditation Research Institute in Rishikesh, India, with participants completing four blocks consisting of two meditation blocks (breath counting and tradition-specific) interspersed with two active thinking blocks. The study investigates neural correlates of meditative states compared to cognitively engaged states.
methods_description: EEG data were collected while participants sat either on the floor or on a chair with eyes closed in a darkened room. Participants performed four experimental blocks alternating between meditation (breath counting and tradition-specific meditation) and active thinking tasks, with block order counterbalanced across participants.
